---
title: "Data Manipulation for GIS  \n A tutorial"
author: " ---- \n *Christophe Bontemps*  \n ![](www/SIAP_logo_2020_1800.png){width=30%}  "
date: "*Version: 1.0 \n (April 2026)*"
output:
  learnr::tutorial: 
    progressive: true
    allow_skip: true
    css: css/tutorial.css
runtime: shiny_prerendered
description: >
  Learn how to manipulate data for GIS, in the same logic than with SQL commands. 
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(learnr)
library(tidyverse)
library(DT)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  comment = ""
)

tutorial_options(
  exercise.timelimit  = 60,
  exercise.lines      = 8,
  exercise.completion = TRUE
)

# ── Embed datasets directly so no CSV files are needed ──────────────────────

districts <- tibble::tribble(
  ~district_id, ~district,                  ~population,
  "D001", "ABAIARA",                  21800,
  "D002", "ACOPIARA",                 54000,
  "D003", "AIUABA",                   17600,
  "D004", "ALTANEIRA",                 7200,
  "D005", "ALTO SANTO",               17800,
  "D006", "ANTONINA DO NORTE",         9500,
  "D007", "ARACATI",                  75000,
  "D008", "ARARIPE",                  22000,
  "D009", "ARNEIROZ",                  8400,
  "D010", "ASSARE",                   24000,
  "D011", "AURORA",                   24800,
  "D012", "BAIXIO",                    7400,
  "D013", "BANABUIU",                 16200,
  "D014", "BARBALHA",                 61000,
  "D015", "BARRO",                    20000,
  "D016", "BOA VIAGEM",               52000,
  "D017", "BREJO SANTO",              43000,
  "D018", "CAMPOS SALES",             29000,
  "D019", "CARIRIACU",                22000,
  "D020", "CARIUS",                   22000,
  "D021", "CATARINA",                 18000,
  "D022", "CEDRO",                    26000,
  "D023", "CRATO",                   125000,
  "D024", "DEP. IRAPUAN PINHEIRO",    12500,
  "D025", "ERERE",                     8000,
  "D026", "FARIAS BRITO",             18000,
  "D027", "GRANJEIRO",                 5100,
  "D028", "IBICUITINGA",              11000,
  "D029", "ICAPUI",                   19000,
  "D030", "ICO",                      66000,
  "D031", "IGUATU",                  102000,
  "D032", "IPAUMIRIM",                12000,
  "D033", "IRACEMA",                  13000,
  "D034", "ITAICABA",                  8000,
  "D035", "ITATIRA",                  18000,
  "D036", "JAGUARETAMA",              19000,
  "D037", "JAGUARIBARA",              10000,
  "D038", "JAGUARIBE",                35000,
  "D039", "JAGUARUANA",               33000,
  "D040", "JARDIM",                   26000,
  "D041", "JATI",                      8700,
  "D042", "JUAZEIRO DO NORTE",       275000,
  "D043", "JUCAS",                    23000,
  "D044", "LAVRAS DA MANGABEIRA",     31000,
  "D045", "LIMOEIRO DO NORTE",        56000,
  "D046", "MADALENA",                 20000,
  "D047", "MAURITI",                  43000,
  "D048", "MILAGRES",                 29000,
  "D049", "MILHA",                    13000,
  "D050", "MISSAO VELHA",             35000,
  "D051", "MOMBACA",                  42000,
  "D052", "MONSENHOR TABOSA",         21000,
  "D053", "MORADA NOVA",              62000,
  "D054", "NOVA OLINDA",              14000,
  "D055", "OROS",                     21000,
  "D056", "PALHANO",                   9500,
  "D057", "PARAMBU",                  38000,
  "D058", "PEDRA BRANCA",             40000,
  "D059", "PENAFORTE",                 7300,
  "D060", "PEREIRO",                  16000,
  "D061", "PIQUET CARNEIRO",          17000,
  "D062", "PORTEIRAS",                14000,
  "D063", "POTENGI",                  13000,
  "D064", "POTIRETAMA",                9000,
  "D065", "QUIXADA",                  88000,
  "D066", "QUIXELO",                  23000,
  "D067", "QUIXERAMOBIM",             79000,
  "D068", "QUIXERE",                  18000,
  "D069", "RUSSAS",                   75000,
  "D070", "SABOEIRO",                 16000,
  "D071", "SALITRE",                  12500,
  "D072", "SANTANA DO CARIRI",        16000,
  "D073", "SAO JOAO DO JAGUARIBE",     8500,
  "D074", "SENADOR POMPEU",           27000,
  "D075", "SOLONOPOLE",               17000,
  "D076", "TABULEIRO DO NORTE",       32000,
  "D077", "TARRAFAS",                 11000,
  "D078", "TAUA",                     56000,
  "D079", "UMARI",                     8500,
  "D080", "VARZEA ALEGRE",            41000
)

agriculture <- tibble::tribble(
  ~district_id, ~year, ~crop,   ~area_ha, ~prod_kg, ~value_real,
  "D001", 2025L, "Rice",    180L,    189L,    67.19,
  "D001", 2025L, "Maize",  1158L,    903L,   221.37,
  "D001", 2025L, "Bean",    532L,    117L,    91.73,
  "D002", 2025L, "Rice",    486L,     61L,    21.68,
  "D002", 2025L, "Maize",  7531L,    595L,   145.86,
  "D002", 2025L, "Bean",   6877L,    540L,   423.38,
  "D003", 2025L, "Rice",     40L,     56L,    19.91,
  "D003", 2025L, "Maize",  6200L,   4595L,  1126.45,
  "D003", 2025L, "Bean",   4670L,    869L,   681.33,
  "D004", 2025L, "Rice",     22L,     14L,     4.98,
  "D004", 2025L, "Maize",   600L,    716L,   175.53,
  "D004", 2025L, "Bean",    350L,    101L,    79.19,
  "D005", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D005", 2025L, "Maize",   290L,     85L,    20.84,
  "D005", 2025L, "Bean",   2224L,   1033L,   809.91,
  "D006", 2025L, "Rice",     76L,     88L,    31.28,
  "D006", 2025L, "Maize",   929L,    352L,    86.29,
  "D006", 2025L, "Bean",    719L,    112L,    87.81,
  "D007", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D007", 2025L, "Maize",  1670L,    645L,   158.12,
  "D007", 2025L, "Bean",   1940L,    567L,   444.55,
  "D008", 2025L, "Rice",    150L,    138L,    49.06,
  "D008", 2025L, "Maize",  4595L,   4806L,  1178.18,
  "D008", 2025L, "Bean",   1800L,    407L,   319.10,
  "D009", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D009", 2025L, "Maize",  2730L,    898L,   220.14,
  "D009", 2025L, "Bean",   2520L,    365L,   286.18,
  "D010", 2025L, "Rice",   1145L,   1073L,   381.44,
  "D010", 2025L, "Maize",  4537L,   2037L,   499.37,
  "D010", 2025L, "Bean",   3125L,    641L,   502.57,
  "D011", 2025L, "Rice",    770L,    832L,   295.77,
  "D011", 2025L, "Maize",  4977L,   3932L,   963.92,
  "D011", 2025L, "Bean",   3565L,    715L,   560.59,
  "D012", 2025L, "Rice",     13L,      6L,     2.13,
  "D012", 2025L, "Maize",   962L,    370L,    90.70,
  "D012", 2025L, "Bean",    560L,     80L,    62.72,
  "D013", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D013", 2025L, "Maize",   800L,    640L,   156.89,
  "D013", 2025L, "Bean",    800L,    326L,   255.60,
  "D014", 2025L, "Rice",    108L,    112L,    39.82,
  "D014", 2025L, "Maize",   312L,    243L,    59.57,
  "D014", 2025L, "Bean",    317L,     94L,    73.70,
  "D015", 2025L, "Rice",    640L,    614L,   218.27,
  "D015", 2025L, "Maize",  4008L,   1178L,   288.78,
  "D015", 2025L, "Bean",   2595L,    535L,   419.46,
  "D016", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D016", 2025L, "Maize", 18678L,   7444L,  1824.88,
  "D016", 2025L, "Bean",  12181L,   2071L,  1623.75,
  "D017", 2025L, "Rice",     65L,     74L,    26.31,
  "D017", 2025L, "Maize",  5160L,   3679L,   901.90,
  "D017", 2025L, "Bean",   2335L,   1013L,   794.23,
  "D018", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D018", 2025L, "Maize",  4137L,   2668L,   654.05,
  "D018", 2025L, "Bean",   2035L,    497L,   389.67,
  "D019", 2025L, "Rice",    675L,    680L,   241.73,
  "D019", 2025L, "Maize",  1350L,    857L,   210.09,
  "D019", 2025L, "Bean",   1306L,    267L,   209.34,
  "D020", 2025L, "Rice",    930L,    494L,   175.61,
  "D020", 2025L, "Maize",  2097L,    885L,   216.96,
  "D020", 2025L, "Bean",   1645L,    335L,   262.65,
  "D021", 2025L, "Rice",     18L,      2L,     0.71,
  "D021", 2025L, "Maize",  1444L,    110L,    26.97,
  "D021", 2025L, "Bean",   1316L,    151L,   118.39,
  "D022", 2025L, "Rice",    330L,    338L,   120.16,
  "D022", 2025L, "Maize",  3063L,   1081L,   265.00,
  "D022", 2025L, "Bean",   2484L,    268L,   210.12,
  "D023", 2025L, "Rice",    640L,    454L,   161.39,
  "D023", 2025L, "Maize",  1993L,   1104L,   270.64,
  "D023", 2025L, "Bean",   1937L,    393L,   308.13,
  "D024", 2025L, "Rice",    320L,    384L,   136.51,
  "D024", 2025L, "Maize",  2142L,   1617L,   396.40,
  "D024", 2025L, "Bean",   1910L,    764L,   599.01,
  "D025", 2025L, "Rice",     54L,     10L,     3.55,
  "D025", 2025L, "Maize",   995L,    437L,   107.13,
  "D025", 2025L, "Bean",    672L,    136L,   106.63,
  "D026", 2025L, "Rice",    815L,    423L,   150.37,
  "D026", 2025L, "Maize",  1580L,    964L,   236.32,
  "D026", 2025L, "Bean",   1900L,    300L,   235.21,
  "D027", 2025L, "Rice",     80L,     73L,    25.95,
  "D027", 2025L, "Maize",   432L,    244L,    59.82,
  "D027", 2025L, "Bean",    260L,     78L,    61.16,
  "D028", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D028", 2025L, "Maize",  1725L,   1725L,   422.88,
  "D028", 2025L, "Bean",   3540L,   2124L,  1665.30,
  "D029", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D029", 2025L, "Maize",   450L,    168L,    41.18,
  "D029", 2025L, "Bean",    600L,    115L,    90.16,
  "D030", 2025L, "Rice",    870L,   2635L,   936.72,
  "D030", 2025L, "Maize",  9530L,   4096L,  1004.12,
  "D030", 2025L, "Bean",   7750L,   1881L,  1474.78,
  "D031", 2025L, "Rice",   4200L,  25143L,  8938.13,
  "D031", 2025L, "Maize",  2796L,    452L,   110.81,
  "D031", 2025L, "Bean",   1936L,    611L,   479.05,
  "D032", 2025L, "Rice",     39L,     19L,     6.75,
  "D032", 2025L, "Maize",  1755L,    693L,   169.89,
  "D032", 2025L, "Bean",   1023L,    136L,   106.63,
  "D033", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D033", 2025L, "Maize",   958L,    321L,    78.69,
  "D033", 2025L, "Bean",    197L,     68L,    53.31,
  "D034", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D034", 2025L, "Maize",   320L,    192L,    47.07,
  "D034", 2025L, "Bean",    435L,    213L,   167.00,
  "D035", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D035", 2025L, "Maize",  3897L,   1072L,   262.80,
  "D035", 2025L, "Bean",   3828L,    668L,   523.74,
  "D036", 2025L, "Rice",     50L,    210L,    74.65,
  "D036", 2025L, "Maize",  1760L,    634L,   155.42,
  "D036", 2025L, "Bean",   4300L,   1100L,   862.45,
  "D037", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D037", 2025L, "Maize",   491L,    185L,    45.35,
  "D037", 2025L, "Bean",   1147L,    329L,   257.95,
  "D038", 2025L, "Rice",     45L,    215L,    76.43,
  "D038", 2025L, "Maize",  1729L,    547L,   134.10,
  "D038", 2025L, "Bean",   2841L,   1234L,   967.51,
  "D039", 2025L, "Rice",    950L,   5700L,  2026.30,
  "D039", 2025L, "Maize",  2500L,   1867L,   457.69,
  "D039", 2025L, "Bean",   1400L,    572L,   448.47,
  "D040", 2025L, "Rice",     60L,     57L,    20.26,
  "D040", 2025L, "Maize",   950L,    609L,   149.29,
  "D040", 2025L, "Bean",    525L,    174L,   136.42,
  "D041", 2025L, "Rice",     12L,     11L,     3.91,
  "D041", 2025L, "Maize",  1740L,   1248L,   305.94,
  "D041", 2025L, "Bean",    994L,    126L,    98.79,
  "D042", 2025L, "Rice",    225L,    221L,    78.56,
  "D042", 2025L, "Maize",   510L,    314L,    76.98,
  "D042", 2025L, "Bean",    482L,    148L,   116.04,
  "D043", 2025L, "Rice",    291L,    232L,    82.47,
  "D043", 2025L, "Maize",  2528L,    689L,   168.91,
  "D043", 2025L, "Bean",   1694L,    350L,   274.41,
  "D044", 2025L, "Rice",   1365L,   2044L,   726.63,
  "D044", 2025L, "Maize",  3391L,   1046L,   256.42,
  "D044", 2025L, "Bean",   2946L,    352L,   275.98,
  "D045", 2025L, "Rice",   1000L,   6000L,  2132.95,
  "D045", 2025L, "Maize",  1060L,   2939L,   720.49,
  "D045", 2025L, "Bean",   1835L,   1356L,  1063.16,
  "D046", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D046", 2025L, "Maize",  8679L,   1197L,   293.44,
  "D046", 2025L, "Bean",   3480L,    355L,   278.33,
  "D047", 2025L, "Rice",    135L,    143L,    50.84,
  "D047", 2025L, "Maize", 16531L,   3571L,   875.42,
  "D047", 2025L, "Bean",   7284L,   1212L,   950.26,
  "D048", 2025L, "Rice",    125L,     88L,    31.28,
  "D048", 2025L, "Maize",  3618L,    644L,   157.88,
  "D048", 2025L, "Bean",   1632L,    175L,   137.21,
  "D049", 2025L, "Rice",    150L,    162L,    57.59,
  "D049", 2025L, "Maize",  4170L,   3336L,   817.81,
  "D049", 2025L, "Bean",   2580L,   1032L,   809.13,
  "D050", 2025L, "Rice",    150L,    140L,    49.77,
  "D050", 2025L, "Maize",  1528L,    390L,    95.61,
  "D050", 2025L, "Bean",   1553L,    358L,   280.69,
  "D051", 2025L, "Rice",     84L,     54L,    19.20,
  "D051", 2025L, "Maize",  5660L,   3288L,   806.05,
  "D051", 2025L, "Bean",   4330L,   1249L,   979.27,
  "D052", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D052", 2025L, "Maize",  4429L,   1085L,   265.99,
  "D052", 2025L, "Bean",   3774L,    437L,   342.63,
  "D053", 2025L, "Rice",   1731L,  10386L,  3692.14,
  "D053", 2025L, "Maize",  1600L,    864L,   211.81,
  "D053", 2025L, "Bean",  10865L,   4477L,  3510.15,
  "D054", 2025L, "Rice",    300L,    188L,    66.83,
  "D054", 2025L, "Maize",  1400L,    851L,   208.62,
  "D054", 2025L, "Bean",   1005L,    166L,   130.15,
  "D055", 2025L, "Rice",    390L,   1598L,   568.08,
  "D055", 2025L, "Maize",  2380L,   1373L,   336.59,
  "D055", 2025L, "Bean",   1080L,    355L,   278.33,
  "D056", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D056", 2025L, "Maize",   259L,    186L,    45.60,
  "D056", 2025L, "Bean",   1155L,    436L,   341.84,
  "D057", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D057", 2025L, "Maize", 13510L,   8228L,  2017.07,
  "D057", 2025L, "Bean",  11100L,   3141L,  2462.67,
  "D058", 2025L, "Rice",    301L,    135L,    47.99,
  "D058", 2025L, "Maize", 14280L,  11424L,  2800.57,
  "D058", 2025L, "Bean",   6070L,   2769L,  2171.01,
  "D059", 2025L, "Rice",     18L,     15L,     5.33,
  "D059", 2025L, "Maize",   920L,    124L,    30.40,
  "D059", 2025L, "Bean",    844L,     61L,    47.83,
  "D060", 2025L, "Rice",     40L,     27L,     9.60,
  "D060", 2025L, "Maize",  3720L,    877L,   214.99,
  "D060", 2025L, "Bean",   3397L,    414L,   324.59,
  "D061", 2025L, "Rice",     20L,      4L,     1.42,
  "D061", 2025L, "Maize",  2811L,   1287L,   315.50,
  "D061", 2025L, "Bean",   1828L,    377L,   295.58,
  "D062", 2025L, "Rice",     21L,     18L,     6.40,
  "D062", 2025L, "Maize",  3300L,    587L,   143.90,
  "D062", 2025L, "Bean",    549L,    249L,   195.23,
  "D063", 2025L, "Rice",     25L,     25L,     8.89,
  "D063", 2025L, "Maize",  3991L,   3076L,   754.07,
  "D063", 2025L, "Bean",   2950L,    664L,   520.60,
  "D064", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D064", 2025L, "Maize",   414L,    125L,    30.64,
  "D064", 2025L, "Bean",   1461L,    255L,   199.93,
  "D065", 2025L, "Rice",      1L,      2L,     0.71,
  "D065", 2025L, "Maize",  5400L,   4320L,  1059.04,
  "D065", 2025L, "Bean",   6800L,   4333L,  3397.25,
  "D066", 2025L, "Rice",   2190L,  12905L,  4587.62,
  "D066", 2025L, "Maize",  1550L,    384L,    94.14,
  "D066", 2025L, "Bean",   1080L,    453L,   355.17,
  "D067", 2025L, "Rice",      1L,      2L,     0.71,
  "D067", 2025L, "Maize",  6000L,   6564L,  1609.15,
  "D067", 2025L, "Bean",   3400L,   1588L,  1245.06,
  "D068", 2025L, "Rice",     30L,    180L,    63.99,
  "D068", 2025L, "Maize",   495L,    495L,   121.35,
  "D068", 2025L, "Bean",    655L,    446L,   349.68,
  "D069", 2025L, "Rice",    150L,    900L,   319.94,
  "D069", 2025L, "Maize",   578L,    416L,   101.98,
  "D069", 2025L, "Bean",   2350L,   1397L,  1095.31,
  "D070", 2025L, "Rice",     41L,     13L,     4.62,
  "D070", 2025L, "Maize",  3000L,    874L,   214.26,
  "D070", 2025L, "Bean",   2163L,    224L,   175.63,
  "D071", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D071", 2025L, "Maize",  3496L,   1779L,   436.12,
  "D071", 2025L, "Bean",   4146L,   1227L,   962.02,
  "D072", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D072", 2025L, "Maize", 13550L,   3835L,   940.14,
  "D072", 2025L, "Bean",  11524L,   1832L,  1436.36,
  "D073", 2025L, "Rice",    130L,    780L,   277.28,
  "D073", 2025L, "Maize",    60L,     14L,     3.43,
  "D073", 2025L, "Bean",    425L,    311L,   243.84,
  "D074", 2025L, "Rice",    120L,    144L,    51.19,
  "D074", 2025L, "Maize",  5838L,   4670L,  1144.84,
  "D074", 2025L, "Bean",   4670L,   1961L,  1537.50,
  "D075", 2025L, "Rice",    235L,    310L,   110.20,
  "D075", 2025L, "Maize",  2748L,   1676L,   410.87,
  "D075", 2025L, "Bean",   2395L,    880L,   689.96,
  "D076", 2025L, "Rice",    200L,   1200L,   426.59,
  "D076", 2025L, "Maize",   730L,     92L,    22.55,
  "D076", 2025L, "Bean",   1340L,    312L,   244.62,
  "D077", 2025L, "Rice",    229L,    229L,    81.41,
  "D077", 2025L, "Maize",  1667L,    745L,   182.63,
  "D077", 2025L, "Bean",   1305L,    239L,   187.39,
  "D078", 2025L, "Rice",     NA,       0L,      NA,
  "D078", 2025L, "Maize", 18473L,  10382L,  2545.12,
  "D078", 2025L, "Bean",  13116L,   2295L,  1799.37,
  "D079", 2025L, "Rice",     10L,      4L,     1.42,
  "D079", 2025L, "Maize",  1068L,    410L,   100.51,
  "D079", 2025L, "Bean",    631L,    103L,    80.76,
  "D080", 2025L, "Rice",   2950L,   3237L,  1150.73,
  "D080", 2025L, "Maize",  3400L,   1650L,   404.49,
  "D080", 2025L, "Bean",   2500L,    395L,   309.70
)
```

## Welcome {data-progressive=FALSE}

### About this tutorial

This tutorial teaches **data manipulation for GIS** using agricultural
production data  (2025). 

Each exercise teaches data manipulation using a syntax (the R ***tidyverse*** syntax)  that is the direct equivalent of a SQL command you may already know:

| SQL | tidyverse verb | What it does |
|-----|----------------|--------------|
| `WHERE` | `filter()` | Keep rows matching a condition |
| `SELECT col1, col2` | `select()` | Keep only certain columns |
| `ORDER BY col DESC` | `arrange(desc())` | Sort rows |
| `GROUP BY` + `SUM()` | `group_by()` + `summarise()` | Aggregate by group |
| `JOIN … ON` | `left_join()` | Combine two tables |

Operations are chained with the **pipe** `%>%` — read it as *"then"*:

```r
agriculture %>%           # start with this table, THEN
  filter(crop == "Rice")  # keep only Rice rows
```

---

### The datasets

Two tables - here two *data frames* - named **`agriculture`** and **`districts`**  are used. They are linked by a unique primary key variable  **`district_id`**. Below is a description of the the two data frames.

**`agriculture`** — 240 rows (3 crops × 80 districts)

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `district_id` 🔑 | text | Links to `districts` |
| `year` | integer | Reference year (2025) |
| `crop` | text | Rice, Maize or Bean |
| `area_ha` | integer | Cultivated area (hectares) |
| `prod_kg` | integer | Production (kilograms) |
| `value_real` | decimal | Economic value (R$) |



```{r preview-data-agri, exercise=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
head(agriculture)
```


**`districts`** — 80 rows

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `district_id` 🔑 | text | Unique ID (D001–D080) |
| `district` | text | District name |
| `population` | integer | Estimated population |


```{r preview-data-districts, exercise=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
head(districts)
```

---

## Exercise 1 · `filter()` {data-progressive=TRUE}

### The concept

`filter()` keeps only the rows that match your conditions — exactly like `WHERE` in SQL.

<div class="compare-wrap">
<div class="sql-box"><div class="box-label">SQL</div>
SELECT * FROM agriculture<br>
WHERE crop = 'Maize'<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;AND prod_kg > 5000;
</div>
<div class="r-box"><div class="box-label">tidyverse</div>
agriculture %>%<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;filter(crop == "Maize",<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;prod_kg > 5000)
</div>
</div>

**Key differences from SQL:**

- Use `==` (double equals) to test equality — `=` alone is assignment in R
- Separate multiple conditions with a **comma** (means AND)
- Text values use `"double quotes"`

### Exercise

From the `agriculture` table, keep only rows where **crop is `"Maize"`** and **`prod_kg` is greater than 5000**.

```{r ex01, exercise=TRUE, exercise.lines=5}
agriculture %>%
  filter(___ == "___",
         ___ > ___)
```

```{r ex01-hint-1}
# The first condition filters on the crop column
agriculture %>%
  filter(crop == "Maize",
         ___ > ___)
```

```{r ex01-hint-2}
# The second condition filters on the prod_kg column
agriculture %>%
  filter(crop == "Maize",
         prod_kg > ___)
```

```{r ex01-solution}
agriculture %>%
  filter(crop == "Maize",
         prod_kg > 5000)
```

### Check your understanding

```{r quiz01}
quiz(
  question(
    "How many rows does the result contain?",
    answer("5"),
    answer("7", correct = TRUE),
    answer("10"),
    answer("12"),
    allow_retry = TRUE,
    random_answer_order = TRUE
  ),
  question(
    "In tidyverse, which operator tests equality in filter()?",
    answer("="),
    answer("==", correct = TRUE),
    answer("<-"),
    answer("%in%"),
    allow_retry = TRUE
  )
)
```

---

## Exercise 2 · `select()` {data-progressive=TRUE}

### The concept

`select()` keeps only the columns you name — like listing column names after `SELECT` in SQL.

<div class="compare-wrap">
<div class="sql-box"><div class="box-label">SQL</div>
SELECT district_id, crop, value_real<br>
FROM agriculture;
</div>
<div class="r-box"><div class="box-label">tidyverse</div>
agriculture %>%<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;select(district_id, crop, value_real)
</div>
</div>

**Key points:**

- Column names are written **without quotes**
- Order in `select()` determines column order in the output
- Use `-col` to *drop* a column instead of keeping it

### Exercise

From the `agriculture` table, keep only the columns **`district_id`**, **`crop`**, and **`value_real`**.

```{r ex02, exercise=TRUE, exercise.lines=4}
agriculture %>%
  select(___, ___, ___)
```

```{r ex02-hint-1}
# Name the three columns separated by commas — no quotes needed
agriculture %>%
  select(district_id, ___, ___)
```

```{r ex02-solution}
agriculture %>%
  select(district_id, crop, value_real)
```

### Check your understanding

```{r quiz02}
quiz(
  question(
    "How many columns does the result have?",
    answer("2"),
    answer("3", correct = TRUE),
    answer("6"),
    answer("240"),
    allow_retry = TRUE
  ),
  question(
    "How would you DROP the 'year' column instead of selecting others?",
    answer("select(-year)", correct = TRUE),
    answer("select(year = FALSE)"),
    answer("remove(year)"),
    answer("filter(year == NA)"),
    allow_retry = TRUE
  )
)
```

---

## Exercise 3 · `arrange()` {data-progressive=TRUE}

### The concept

`arrange()` sorts rows — like `ORDER BY` in SQL. Wrap a column name in `desc()` to sort descending.

<div class="compare-wrap">
<div class="sql-box"><div class="box-label">SQL</div>
SELECT * FROM agriculture<br>
ORDER BY value_real DESC;
</div>
<div class="r-box"><div class="box-label">tidyverse</div>
agriculture %>%<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;arrange(desc(value_real))
</div>
</div>

**Key points:**

- Default is ascending — `arrange(col)` = `ORDER BY col ASC`
- Descending requires wrapping: `arrange(desc(col))` = `ORDER BY col DESC`
- Sort by multiple columns: `arrange(crop, desc(value_real))`

### Exercise

Sort the `agriculture` table by **`value_real`** from **highest to lowest**.

```{r ex03, exercise=TRUE, exercise.lines=4}
agriculture %>%
  arrange(___(___))
```

```{r ex03-hint-1}
# Use desc() inside arrange() for descending order
agriculture %>%
  arrange(desc(___))
```

```{r ex03-solution}
agriculture %>%
  arrange(desc(value_real))
```

### Check your understanding

```{r quiz03}
quiz(
  question(
    "Which district and crop appears in the first row?",
    answer("D031 — Rice", correct = TRUE),
    answer("D021 — Rice"),
    answer("D066 — Maize"),
    answer("D080 — Bean"),
    allow_retry = TRUE,
    random_answer_order = TRUE
  ),
  question(
    "How would you sort by crop alphabetically, then by prod_kg descending within each crop?",
    answer("arrange(crop, desc(prod_kg))", correct = TRUE),
    answer("arrange(desc(crop), prod_kg)"),
    answer("arrange(crop) %>% arrange(desc(prod_kg))"),
    answer("order_by(crop, -prod_kg)"),
    allow_retry = TRUE
  )
)
```

---

## Exercise 4 · `group_by()` + `summarise()` {data-progressive=TRUE}

### The concept

`group_by()` defines groups, then `summarise()` computes one value per group — equivalent to `GROUP BY` + aggregate functions in SQL.

<div class="compare-wrap">
<div class="sql-box"><div class="box-label">SQL</div>
SELECT district_id,<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;SUM(prod_kg) AS total_prod_kg<br>
FROM agriculture<br>
GROUP BY district_id;
</div>
<div class="r-box"><div class="box-label">tidyverse</div>
agriculture %>%<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;group_by(district_id) %>%<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;summarise(total_prod_kg =<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;sum(prod_kg, na.rm = TRUE))
</div>
</div>

**Key points:**

- Always use `na.rm = TRUE` inside `sum()` — otherwise a single `NA` makes the whole sum `NA`
- Common aggregates: `sum()`, `mean()`, `n()` (count rows), `n_distinct()`, `max()`, `min()`
- `group_by()` alone changes nothing visible — it only takes effect in the next `summarise()` or `mutate()`

### Exercise

For each **`district_id`**, calculate the **total production** (sum of `prod_kg`). Name the result column **`total_prod_kg`**.

```{r ex04, exercise=TRUE, exercise.lines=6}
agriculture %>%
  group_by(___) %>%
  summarise(total_prod_kg = ___(prod_kg, na.rm = TRUE))
```

```{r ex04-hint-1}
# group_by() takes the column you want to group on
agriculture %>%
  group_by(district_id) %>%
  summarise(total_prod_kg = ___(prod_kg, na.rm = TRUE))
```

```{r ex04-hint-2}
# Use sum() to add up all values within each group
agriculture %>%
  group_by(district_id) %>%
  summarise(total_prod_kg = sum(prod_kg, na.rm = TRUE))
```

```{r ex04-solution}
agriculture %>%
  group_by(district_id) %>%
  summarise(total_prod_kg = sum(prod_kg, na.rm = TRUE))
```

### Check your understanding

```{r quiz04}
quiz(
  question(
    "How many rows does the result have?",
    answer("3"),
    answer("80", correct = TRUE),
    answer("240"),
    answer("160"),
    allow_retry = TRUE
  ),
  question(
    "Why do we write na.rm = TRUE inside sum()?",
    answer("To remove the grouping after summarise()"),
    answer("Because sum() needs it to work at all"),
    answer("To avoid returning NA when any value in the group is missing", correct = TRUE),
    answer("To round the result to integers"),
    allow_retry = TRUE
  )
)
```

---

## Exercise 5 · `left_join()` {data-progressive=TRUE}

### The concept

`left_join()` adds columns from a second table by matching on a shared key — equivalent to `JOIN … ON` in SQL.

<div class="compare-wrap">
<div class="sql-box"><div class="box-label">SQL</div>
SELECT d.district, a.crop,<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;a.prod_kg, d.population<br>
FROM agriculture a<br>
JOIN districts d<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;ON a.district_id = d.district_id;
</div>
<div class="r-box"><div class="box-label">tidyverse</div>
agriculture %>%<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;left_join(districts,<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;by = "district_id") %>%<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;select(district, crop,<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;prod_kg, population)
</div>
</div>

**Key points:**

- `left_join()` keeps **all rows from the left table** and adds matching columns from the right
- The `by =` argument names the shared key column — must match exactly in both tables
- Chain `select()` afterwards to keep only the columns you need

### Exercise

Join `agriculture` and `districts` using **`district_id`** as the key. Then keep only the columns **`district`**, **`crop`**, **`prod_kg`**, and **`population`**.

```{r ex05, exercise=TRUE, exercise.lines=7}
agriculture %>%
  left_join(___, by = "___") %>%
  select(___, ___, ___, ___)
```

```{r ex05-hint-1}
# The second table to join is districts; the key is district_id
agriculture %>%
  left_join(districts, by = "district_id") %>%
  select(___, ___, ___, ___)
```

```{r ex05-hint-2}
# Select the four columns: district (from districts), crop, prod_kg, population
agriculture %>%
  left_join(districts, by = "district_id") %>%
  select(district, crop, prod_kg, population)
```

```{r ex05-solution}
agriculture %>%
  left_join(districts, by = "district_id") %>%
  select(district, crop, prod_kg, population)
```

### Check your understanding

```{r quiz05}
quiz(
  question(
    "How many rows does the joined result have?",
    answer("80"),
    answer("160"),
    answer("240", correct = TRUE),
    answer("320"),
    allow_retry = TRUE
  ),
  question(
    "What does left_join() do when a district_id in agriculture has no match in districts?",
    answer("It removes that row from the result"),
    answer("It stops with an error"),
    answer("It keeps the row and fills the districts columns with NA", correct = TRUE),
    answer("It duplicates the row"),
    allow_retry = TRUE
  )
)
```

---

## Bonus · Combining verbs {data-progressive=TRUE}

### Real analysis chains multiple verbs

Run each chunk to see how verbs combine. Feel free to modify them!

**Top 10 Bean-producing districts by name:**

```{r bonus1, exercise=TRUE, exercise.lines=9}
agriculture %>%
  filter(crop == "Bean") %>%
  group_by(district_id) %>%
  summarise(total_prod_kg = sum(prod_kg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_prod_kg)) %>%
  left_join(districts, by = "district_id") %>%
  select(district, total_prod_kg) %>%
  head(10)
```

**Total production and value per crop:**

```{r bonus2, exercise=TRUE, exercise.lines=8}
agriculture %>%
  group_by(crop) %>%
  summarise(
    total_prod_kg = sum(prod_kg,    na.rm = TRUE),
    total_value   = sum(value_real, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_districts   = n_distinct(district_id)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_value))
```

**Bar chart — total production by crop:**

```{r bonus3, exercise=TRUE, exercise.lines=15}
agriculture %>%
  group_by(crop) %>%
  summarise(total_prod_kg = sum(prod_kg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(crop, total_prod_kg),
             y = total_prod_kg,
             fill = crop)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Bean = "#a78bfa", Maize = "#f5a623", Rice = "#3ecf8e")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(title = "Total production by crop 2025",
       x = NULL, y = "Production (kg)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

---

## Summary · SQL → tidyverse cheatsheet {data-progressive=FALSE}

```{r cheatsheet, echo=FALSE}
tibble::tribble(
  ~SQL,                           ~tidyverse,                                    ~Notes,
  "SELECT col1, col2",            "select(col1, col2)",                          "No quotes on column names",
  "SELECT *",                     "(default — omit select)",                     "All columns by default",
  "SELECT DISTINCT col",          "distinct(col)",                               "Removes duplicate rows",
  "WHERE col = 'x'",              "filter(col == 'x')",                          "== for equality",
  "WHERE col > 5 AND col2 = 'y'", "filter(col > 5, col2 == 'y')",               "Comma means AND",
  "WHERE col IN ('a','b')",       "filter(col %in% c('a','b'))",                 "Use %in% for lists",
  "WHERE col IS NULL",            "filter(is.na(col))",                          "NA not NULL in R",
  "ORDER BY col ASC",             "arrange(col)",                                "Ascending is default",
  "ORDER BY col DESC",            "arrange(desc(col))",                          "Wrap in desc()",
  "GROUP BY col",                 "group_by(col)",                               "Pair with summarise()",
  "SUM(col) AS total",            "summarise(total = sum(col, na.rm=TRUE))",     "na.rm drops NAs",
  "COUNT(*) AS n",                "summarise(n = n())",                          "n() counts rows in group",
  "COUNT(DISTINCT col)",          "summarise(n = n_distinct(col))",              "Unique values per group",
  "JOIN t2 ON t1.k = t2.k",      "left_join(t2, by = 'k')",                    "Keeps all left rows",
  "SELECT col, col*2 AS double",  "mutate(double = col * 2)",                    "mutate() adds new columns"
) %>%
  knitr::kable(col.names = c("SQL", "tidyverse", "Notes"),
               align = c("l","l","l"))
```

---

*Dataset: Agricultural production, — 2025.*  
*Packages: `tidyverse` (dplyr, ggplot2, readr), `DT`.*
